verification_gate: All child tasks complete with passing proof records; no child draft; lane acceptance satisfied.

gate_result.children_evidence: ARCHBP-065 (flake package, 2/2), ARCHBP-066 (enter lifecycle, 4/4), ARCHBP-067 (zero-leak teardown, 3/3), ARCHBP-068 (durable binds, 3/3), ARCHBP-069 (resource passthrough, 3/3), ARCHBP-070 (multi-envelope, 1/1), and ARCHBP-071 (acceptance, 4/4) each carry a passing revision-1 proof record in this ledger.
gate_result.draft_evidence: All seven children went from an executed red (engine absent, 7/7 gate files failing) to green (20/20) in one lane delivery; none is draft or simulated.
gate_result.acceptance_evidence: The bubblewrap user-namespace hermetic envelope exists as a nix-declared yazelix flake app (PR #103), proven live on the target host: private namespaces with a tmpfs home overlay, declared-env injection, nu-only default shell, durable meta/var and postgres-datadir binds with write persistence, on-demand GPU/port/device acquire+release, N concurrent envelopes without collision, zero-leak teardown, published docs, and recorded T1 invariant conformance — with the AppArmor executor constraint recorded honestly rather than silently downgraded.
gate_result.full_suite: Lane specs 20/20 across seven gate files.
gate_result.product_execution_evidence: Every child gate executes real envelopes (bwrap) or the real nix build; the rollup accepts only executed child proofs.

proof_summary: ARCHBP-050 is complete: the bubblewrap user-namespace hermetic envelope lane rolled up with all seven children (ARCHBP-065..071) complete and green — a nix-declared envelope engine (yazelix PR #103) proven live for namespace hermeticity, durable-plane binds, on-demand resource acquire/release, multi-envelope coexistence, zero-leak teardown, and T1 invariant conformance, with honest executor-downgrade recording on AppArmor-restricted hosts.
